claim_boundary.allowed: sealed live fold-switch solution packet for RfaH-CTD mechanism language, pending review
claim_boundary.not_allowed: universal protein-folding solved claim, atomic transition-pathway solved claim, or single-consensus-fold claim

falsification_criteria: RfaH-CTD reproducibly has one context-independent consensus fold across NTD-bound and released contexts
removing NTD-bound context does not change alpha-hairpin/autoinhibition grammar
released CTD context does not support beta-state or translation-coupling grammar
generic transcription-factor annotation alone predicts the full RfaH packet
IDP phase-separation or membrane-channel grammar explains the packet better than fold switching
coordinate evidence before sealing is required for the packet
independent holdouts contradict more than two explicit perturbation predictions

low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.atomic_claim: blocked; no coordinate or atomic pathway solution is claimed
low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.beta_state: released RfaH-CTD beta-barrel or beta-roll translation-coupling grammar
low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.transition_model: context-dependent refolding pathway with NTD and partner control; atomic transition coordinates are not claimed

operator_region_rationale.CTD_release_switch_operator: The solution requires a release event that makes a second CTD state available without claiming an atomic path.
operator_region_rationale.NTD_bound_autoinhibition_operator: UniProt and mechanism literature describe the NTD cavity as masked by the CTD before activation context.
operator_region_rationale.alpha_helical_hairpin_state_operator: Allowed state labels indicate the CTD can occupy an alpha-helical hairpin state when packed against the NTD.
operator_region_rationale.beta_barrel_or_beta_roll_state_operator: Allowed state labels indicate a released CTD beta-barrel/beta-roll translation-factor state.
operator_region_rationale.no_single_consensus_fold_operator: A single consensus fold would erase the experimentally relevant alpha/beta state separation.
operator_region_rationale.partner_context_refolding_operator: The same CTD region must be interpreted through its partner context rather than through one static fold.
operator_region_rationale.transcription_activation_context_operato: RfaH recruitment to ops/RNAP and antitermination function define the transcription-side context.
operator_region_rationale.translation_coupling_context_operator: Translation activator annotations and RfaH fold-switch literature place the released CTD in a translation-coupling role.

operator_regions.rationale: Allowed state labels indicate the CTD can occupy an alpha-helical hairpin state when packed against the NTD.
Allowed state labels indicate a released CTD beta-barrel/beta-roll translation-factor state.
operator_regions.span: CTD residues 101-162 after release from NTD context

operator_regions.rationale: UniProt and mechanism literature describe the NTD cavity as masked by the CTD before activation context.
operator_regions.span: NTD residues 1-100 coupled to CTD residues 101-162

operator_regions.rationale: The solution requires a release event that makes a second CTD state available without claiming an atomic path.
RfaH recruitment to ops/RNAP and antitermination function define the transcription-side context.
Translation activator annotations and RfaH fold-switch literature place the released CTD in a translation-coupling role.
The same CTD region must be interpreted through its partner context rather than through one static fold.
A single consensus fold would erase the experimentally relevant alpha/beta state separation.

perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: must be rejected as a wrong mechanism class

perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: must be rejected as a wrong mechanism class
supports switch-path grammar but not an atomic pathway-solved claim
weakens translation-coupling grammar without erasing transcription antitermination context
biases toward alpha/autoinhibited grammar and delays or reduces beta-state release

predicted_context_switches.predicted_shift: CTD release becomes available; transcription antitermination grammar is active
beta-barrel or beta-roll translation-coupling grammar dominates
insufficient; full fold-switch packet must abstain
blocked because it destroys state separation

predicted_fold_switch_grammar.prediction: NTD-bound RfaH favors an alpha-hairpin CTD grammar that masks or stabilizes the autoinhibited interface.
Release of the CTD favors a beta-barrel/beta-roll grammar compatible with translation-coupling partner context.
The ops/RNAP activation context shifts the NTD/CTD interface and gates access to the released CTD state.
Partner labels are causal inputs: NTD-bound, RNAP/ops-bound, and S10/ribosome contexts should not collapse to the same grammar.
Intermediate evidence can support a switch-path model but cannot justify an atomic pathway-solved claim.

proposed_experimental_tests: compare CTD secondary-structure signatures in isolated, NTD-bound, and released/partner contexts using non-coordinate spectroscopy
mutate or weaken NTD-CTD interface residues and measure alpha-state loss versus beta-state gain
test released CTD binding to S10/ribosome-context partners and measure translation-coupling readouts
force alpha- or beta-stabilizing substitutions and verify context-dependent functional tradeoffs
measure kinetic intermediates after CTD release without using them as atomic-pathway proof
run negative controls with generic transcription-factor and membrane-channel annotations only
